methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $125M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M+

data.narrative:
Freeform, a Series B robotics and manufacturing company, has raised $125M+ to date. Valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 9 private and 1 public company in the robotics and manufacturing sector, including peers such as 1X Technologies ($10.0B), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B), Dexterity ($1.6B), Collaborative Robotics ($500M), and Fairmat ($350M).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $125M raised proxy, yielding $238M. Peer-median analysis across the six same-sector, same-stage comparables produces a median valuation of $1.1B, with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B, reflecting material heterogeneity in the peer set.

The two methods triangulate to a range of $202M (low) to $1.2B (high), with a median of $656M. The wide dispersion—a 6x spread between low and high—reflects meaningful uncertainty around Freeform's positioning within the robotics landscape. Stage-progression suggests a more conservative read anchored to capital deployed, while peer-median reflects the elevated valuations commanded by mature Series B robotics businesses, particularly leaders like 1X Technologies. Key uncertainties include Freeform's relative technology differentiation, margin profile, and path to revenue relative to the peer cohort.
